key_insight: A fixed-budget, cluster-aware evaluation protocol can substantially change which RAG methods appear to win, revealing that standard LLM-as-a-judge comparisons may overstate progress.
engineering_relevance: For AI materials science and other multi-hop RAG settings, the paper provides a more reliable measurement standard for comparing retrieval strategies under identical resource constraints.
method: Fix the candidate pool to top-100 and hold evidence budget, answer cap, generator, and prompt constant.
Use pre-registered hypotheses with cluster-aware inference, plus an exact cluster sign-flip check when feasible.
Add second-judge replication to reduce judge-specific noise and improve robustness.
evidence_signal: On 400 multi-hop questions in CS/ML and Materials Science, a simple binomial test made all four semantic-baseline comparisons look significant, but cluster-aware inference left only one Bonferroni-significant result.
watch_item: The protocol is stricter and more realistic, but exact cluster sign-flip testing may be infeasible in some datasets or evaluation pipelines.
paper_title: A Fixed-Budget, Cluster-Aware Standard for LLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation: A Multi-Hop RAG Stress Test
